concepts.title: Why schemes aren't enough
concepts.blurb: Some natural quotients (e.g. $\mathbb{A}^1 / \mathbb{Z}$ over a field, or non-free group actions on schemes) are not schemes. Algebraic spaces are the smallest enlargement of schemes that closes the category under such étale quotients while keeping a representable definition.

concepts.title: Étale equivalence relations
concepts.blurb: An étale equivalence relation on a scheme $U$ is a monomorphism $R \hookrightarrow U \times U$ which is an equivalence relation in the categorical sense, with both projections étale. Quotients of these are exactly algebraic spaces.

concepts.title: Algebraic spaces as étale sheaves
concepts.blurb: An algebraic space is a sheaf $X$ on the étale site of schemes whose diagonal $X \to X \times X$ is representable and which admits an étale surjection $U \twoheadrightarrow X$ from a scheme. Equivalently, $X = U/R$ for an étale equivalence relation $R \rightrightarrows U$.

concepts.title: Morphisms of algebraic spaces
concepts.blurb: Morphisms of algebraic spaces are morphisms of étale sheaves; étale-locally on the source and target they reduce to scheme morphisms. Most scheme-morphism properties (étale, smooth, proper, finite type) extend verbatim.

concepts.title: Examples: free quotients and a non-scheme
concepts.blurb: Any scheme is an algebraic space. The free $\mathbb{Z}$-action on $\mathbb{A}^1$ over a field has quotient an algebraic space that is not a scheme (no separated quasi-projective representative exists). Hironaka's example gives a smooth proper algebraic space over $\mathbb{C}$ that isn't a scheme.

concepts.title: Algebraic spaces sit between schemes and DM stacks
concepts.blurb: An algebraic space is a stack with trivial automorphism groups; a DM stack with trivial automorphism groups is an algebraic space. The hierarchy schemes ⊂ algebraic spaces ⊂ DM stacks ⊂ Artin stacks tracks how much non-trivial isotropy the geometric object can carry.
